title: Implement dashboard Gravatar integration
description:
Implementation task for the Gravatar avatar feature.

Expected work:
- Locate current sidebar user identity and user settings components.
- Add a single shared way to derive Gravatar avatar URLs from the authenticated user email.
- Render the Gravatar in the left sidebar behind/alongside the user name according to existing UI conventions.
- Add user settings affordance that links users to Gravatar profile management for updates.
- Add focused tests/specs for URL derivation, sidebar rendering, and settings link behavior.

Notes:
- Do not add a direct avatar upload path.
- Do not add provider fallback chains unless explicitly requested.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Design note: no Gravatar API key is needed for the first implementation. The scoped requirement is avatar image display plus a settings link; avatar images can use the public Gravatar image URL built from the normalized email SHA-256 hash. REST profile enrichment and Quick Editor can remain out of scope unless requested.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Research summary: Gravatar avatar images are served as normal HTTPS image URLs from the normalized email SHA-256 hash, with size/default/rating query parameters. API keys/Bearer tokens apply to the REST profile API and richer account/profile access, not to basic avatar image rendering. Recommended first slice: keyless avatar URL helper, authenticated user attribute hook, sidebar AvatarImage replacement for the hard-coded RP/Ryan Porter row, and a settings card linking to Gravatar avatar management. Quick Editor remains optional future scope.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Schema verification: Amplify Gen2 auth is configured with email login only, and the data schema has Account plus domain models but no User/UserProfile model. Current app-level state has account selection, not persisted per-user profile data. Gravatar first slice should read Cognito email at runtime rather than add a Users table.
